body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: proof-on-close honesty gate
body.intention: By the gate date, confirm proof-on-close raised build honesty without stalling throughput or driving turtles-to-dust on exploration.
body.description:
**What changed:** Closing a load-bearing ticket now requires a HEAD-bound proof artifact, certified for sufficiency by an independent Evaluator; intention replaces goal as the tracked unit; CP2-CP6 consume as tags.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) throughput drag — per-ticket gate-writing cost lands up-front; watch tickets stalling at close because no gate is writable (may signal the intention was never falsifiable). (2) scope creep of 'load-bearing' — if everything gets called load-bearing, exploration drowns in proof tax (turtles-to-dust). (3) Evaluator becomes a bottleneck/cost sink if panel-certify fires on non-load-bearing closes. (4) builders gaming the gate with loose tests the certifier still passes (the cured bug resurfacing one level up). (5) shipped-unproven escape overused to dodge the gate. (6) the load-bearing dependence-classifier misclassifies (false-exploration lets hollow code through; false-load-bearing taxes throwaways).
**Signals to watch:** count of closes blocked for missing/stale proof; ratio load-bearing vs shipped-unproven; Evaluator certify latency/cost; any closed load-bearing ticket whose proof grep shows no HEAD-bound proof (gate leak); recurrence of hollow-build complaints despite green proofs (loose-test resurfacing); misclassification reports.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-07-04 or when the first 10 load-bearing tickets have closed under the new gate.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
